failedAssertions.reason: The fix correctly removed file-based fallback and now uses `cursor-agent status` for auth detection. /health correctly shows 'not_authenticated' when cursor-agent is not logged in. However, the underlying architectural issue remains: cursor-agent stores tokens internally and not in ~/.cursor/ files. Cannot verify authenticated case without logged-in cursor-agent.

cli-config.json does not contain an access token - only user settings (permissions, editor, model preferences). cursor-agent stores tokens internally, not in files accessible to the proxy. This is an architectural limitation that cannot be resolved - cursor-agent's auth mechanism does not expose tokens to external processes.

blockedAssertions.blockedBy: Cannot test quota exceeded without triggering real API usage limits. Would require actual Cursor API quota consumption.

Cannot test 401 for missing auth because: (1) model validation now happens before auth check (fix for VAL-ERROR-003), (2) without authenticated cursor-agent, cannot determine which models are valid to test auth with a known model. Requires authenticated cursor-agent to establish valid model list.
